title: Tapping to Enjoy Tracking Money
subtitle: A tapping practice for releasing dread around account reviews and spreadsheets
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 51 of a 12-week money program. The script targets the avoidance of checking accounts and tracking spending, and reframes money management as something that can feel satisfying rather than draining.
sourceNotes: The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s. The script for this practice is adapted from Day 51 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025, ISBN 9798268793796).

practice.whenToUse: Use before sitting down with your accounts or when you find yourself putting off a money review.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice from Day 51 of a 12-week money program. The script works with the habit of avoiding account checks and treating money management as something to get through rather than something to enjoy. The reframe moves toward tracking as a satisfying part of daily money care.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop
Even though I used to dread looking at numbers, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I've avoided budgets and spreadsheets, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though money admin once felt heavy, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
Eyebrow: I've put off reviewing accounts.
Side of eye: I felt nervous about what I'd see.
Under eye: I saw money management as boring.
Under nose: I avoided tracking to dodge stress.
Chin: I sometimes felt out of control.
Collarbone: I'm ready to change that.
Under arm: I welcome a lighter way to handle money.
Top of head: I allow money management to feel good.
Reframe to positive
Eyebrow: I enjoy checking my accounts.
Side of eye: I like watching balances grow.
Under eye: I track with curiosity and ease.
Under nose: I celebrate the progress I see.
Chin: I feel calm and organised with my finances.
Collarbone: I make money management simple and enjoyable.
Under arm: I feel empowered by knowing my numbers.
Top of head: I enjoy caring for money every day.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy. The script is adapted from Day 51 of MONEY: A 12-Week Tapping Program to Attract Cash, Overflow & Abundance Without Hustle or Burnout (Rebecca J Page, 2025).
